newsSinceJun6: No company-specific material news since June 6. Today's -5.26% drop is macro/flow-driven: the May jobs report pushed the 30-year Treasury yield above 5%, pressuring AI infrastructure and grid-exposed manufacturers on fears of deferred capex and a Fed that hikes rather than cuts. Standing fundamentals unchanged: Q2 FY26 beat ($4.01B revenue, $3.16 non-GAAP EPS), raised FY26 revenue guide to $13.7-14.3B, $600M buyback, ZT Systems integration adding ~$5-6B annual revenue, BofA PT raised to $280.

sources.label: StockStory: Sanmina and Methode Electronics Shares Plummet (June 9 rate-driven sell-off)
Yahoo Finance: Why Sanmina Stock Just Got Sacked
StockTitan 8-K: Sanmina lifts 2026 outlook and adds $600M buyback

verdicts.rankRationale: A -5.26% one-day sell-off driven purely by the 30-year yield breaching 5% — not by any crack in the AI/cloud integration thesis — trims the rerating premium this alpha page penalizes and modestly improves entry asymmetry versus yesterday's hold at a higher price.
verdicts.bull: Raised FY26 guide ($13.7-14.3B), $600M buyback, and ZT Systems adding $5-6B of hyperscaler AI rack revenue keep Sanmina a direct cluster-scale integration bottleneck; rate-driven dips into an intact thesis are buyable de-crowding events.
verdicts.neutral: Still up ~50% YTD and +173% over a year on a low-margin EMS model; one macro down-day does not undo the rerating, so it stays in the crowded/rerated bucket rather than returning to top residual-alpha territory.
verdicts.bear: If the 30-year holds above 5% and the Fed tilts hawkish, data center and grid customers stretch order cycles, and a 3.9% GAAP operating-margin assembler with a rich multiple has little cushion against AI capex digestion.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q3 FY26 results in late July with ZT Systems revenue ramp evidence and any further hyperscaler rack-integration wins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Sustained long-end rates above 5% deferring data center infrastructure capex and compressing EMS multiples back toward historical norms.
